title: calorophus gracilis
intro: Calorophus gracilis, also known as the Mexican gold poppy or hummingbird flower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Calorophus gracilis include its potential to soothe digestive issues and alleviate respiratory problems, as well as its ability to reduce fever and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
